entry.title: Re: Shared row axis in two tables in SAP Analytics Cloud...
entry.content: Share row axis is not supported in SAC. But for your requirement, you can simply create a restricted measure( restrict to period with navigation to year to date) and display with periodic measure side by side.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Test Automation Tool using in RISE...
entry.content:
Yes, 
SAP Test Automation Tool is only available for S/4Hana public Cloud.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Is it truly a limi...
entry.content: Is all your 250,000 members part of the same hierarchy? As far as I understand, you should be fine if system doesn't throw you any error/warning message.

entry.title: Re: AFO SAC Edition and Standard Edition installed simult...
entry.content: You don't have to.  AfO standard edition covers all the features AfO SAC edition provides you.

entry.title: Re: Configuring Multiple Communication Configurations wit...
entry.content:
Dear Katja,

The way would be to use router in CI to call the necessary iflow.

The router in SAP Cloud Integration (CI) is a filter that routes messages to the right destinations based on specific conditions. The router can be used to: Speed up integration, Control message flows, and Improve error handling.

Communication arrangement is called based on the business partner assigned to the communication arrangement and not on BRF+.

So if document 1 has business partner A, the communication arrangement its linked to will be called

if Document 2 has business partner B, then communication arrangement its linked to will be called.

Regards

Sahil Grover

__PRESENT

entry.title: Re: Can we integrate external API of Cleartax for EInvoic...

entry.title: SAW 17: Question on JSON parsing (dbo.sp_parse_json procedure)
entry.content:
Hi,

SAP SQL AnyWhere 17.0.10.

There is some JSON packet. I pass it to the procedure "sp_parse_json" for processing

declare cJSON long varchar;
call dbo.sp_parse_json('sql_array', cJSON);

I will get the parsed JSON package into the variable "sql_array".

Question: How do I check "sql_array" for the presence of a certain field in a JSON packet? JSON-package, whether this field is there or not ?

entry.title: VF31: how to excluce cancelled billing documents
entry.content:
hi,

in VF31 the billing documents that were cancelled via VF11 are still showing up. How can they be excluded?

The cancelation document is not showing up, but the original is. How can we avoid this?

kr

Nico

Thanks

Sunandha

entry.title: CSRF Token Missing Error in Android OData Service
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m currently facing an issue with my application. It works perfectly in Cloud Foundry (Web), but when I deploy it in Mobile Services (Android), I encounter a “CSRF token is missing” error when posting data.

I’m working on an Android application that interacts with an OData service, and I need guidance on retrieving the CSRF token. I understand that CSRF protection is crucial for secure API calls, but I’m unsure how to implement this in my Android app.

Could anyone provide a step-by-step approach or share code snippets on how to obtain the CSRF token for an OData service? I’ve checked my configurations and ensured that CSRF protection is enabled, but the problem persists.

Has anyone else experienced this issue? Any insights or solutions would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance for your help!

entry.title: Questions about the S/4HANA build process
entry.content:
Question.
I am currently looking to build an on-premise environment (TDD version) of S/4 HANA for our company, but we are having trouble understanding the steps to build it.
In the service license document, it says to create a license key and download the software from Manage My Services, but I don't know what license key and software correspond to the service (SAP Test and Demo User, starter package) that is currently activated.
I also do not know how to use the license key, so I would appreciate it if you could tell me how to use it.

Current status
I have a contract with SAP Test and Demo User, starter package (TD_7016852),
I am trying to build TDD version of S/4HANA on-prem environment.
I am using SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP 6 as the operating system and have the environment to install the software.

I am also trying things out about the license key and software,
The license key is
Product of Installation Number : SAP S/4 HANA
Add New System
Product SAP S/4 HANA
Version SAP S/4 HANA, on-premise edition 1511
as a license key.

In addition, as the software
SAP S/4 HANA / SAP S/4 HANA 2023/
SAP HANA PLATFORM EDITION 2.0 (INSTALLATIONS AND UPGRADES)
I am trying to install the software.

I have already confirmed that I can download the software from the SAP Download Manager regarding the software installation.
I would greatly appreciate it if someone could give me an answer to my question.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) - JDBC Adapter - Step-by-step guide - Pre-requisites and Configurati
entry.content:
Hi @ChingHong , 

I am using the same approach to insert more 100k record with per batch 10k records, but the problem is with the Non-Atomic Mode, even though I am using the Non-Atomic mode, for large value constraints failure it is falling whole operation. i.e. even in 10K record if one record is failed due to the length constraints it is unable to insert all 10k record. though it is perfectly working for the duplicate constraints case i.e. in 10k Records if multiple records failed due to the duplicate entry error, successful record it is able to insert. So my question is does Non_atomic only work for the duplicate entry error ?

  • What is the best and correct practice for assigning key user extensibilities to different kind of software collections?

entry.title: SAP Solution Manager のデータが最新であることを確認方法
entry.content:
2.SAP Solution Manager のフォローアップ活動の実行

下記項目の実施内容が分からず相談した次第です。
[項目]
・SAP Solution Manager のデータが最新であることを確認します
[手順]
・システムデータのチェック
[実施内容]
システムランドスケープディレクトリ (SLD) で、システムランドスケープが最新です。これを行うには、最新のソフトウェアコンポーネントかどうかを確認します。

上記実施にあたり、参考になるNote等あればご教示いただくことは可能でしょうか。

以上よろしくお願いいたします。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Solution Manager のデータが最新であることを確認方法
entry.content:
ご担当者　様

平素よりお世話になっております。

お問い合わせ頂いたSAP Solution Manager のデータが最新であることを確認するには、SAP Solution Manager／LMDBにて登録済の管理対象システムの情報が最新かどうかをご確認頂きたいことという認識が合っていましょうか。

それでは、下記のステップに従ってSolution Manager LMDBで管理対象システムの詳細をご覧下さい。

トランザクションLMDB -> 管理対象システムSID（Solution ManagerのSIDで入力してもOK） -> システム概要  -> Detail(詳細) -> データサプライヤー情報で下記の情報をご確認できます。

  • 最終手動変更記録 
  • データサプライヤーによる最終変更 
  • 作成日時 
  • データサプライヤー一覧

ご不明な点等ございましたらお知らせください。

以上、宜しくお願いいたします。

SAPサポート

entry.title: Re: error in cl_sxml_string_writer, sXML Library
entry.content:
Due to the discussion here, our development has introduced the following enhancements with releases 9.14/2502, 8.16/2025:

Header Options for sXML Library

Header options for the XML declaration are now available in the sXML Library:

  • CO_OPT_VAL_NO (no XML header), this is the default option.
  • CO_OPT_VAL_WITHOUT_ENCODING (version)
  • CO_OPT_VAL_WITHOUT_ENCODING (version and encoding)

But regarding the debugger problem, I don't have any further information yet. You might report it as a customer incident.

entry.title: SAP Goods consumption document posting date should be same as POD confirmation
entry.content:

We are implementing the POD process and have identified an issue during the process.

POD Date does not match voucher posting date

Could you tell me what caused this?

Is there a good solution?

Await your kind feedback

Thanks

entry.title: setup windows cluster for SAP HA
entry.content:
I have problem when setting Windows cluster before installation SAP Netware 7.52. please help.

Could you please share cluster requirements/reparation for installing sap HA?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: setup windows cluster for SAP HA
entry.content:
Setting up a High Availability (HA) cluster for SAP systems involves careful planning and adherence to specific requirements. Below are the general steps and requirements for preparing a cluster environment for SAP HA:

1. Hardware Requirements

  • Servers: At least two servers are needed for a basic HA setup to ensure redundancy.
  • Storage: Shared storage (like SAN) is required to store the SAP database and central services. The storage must support clustering.
  • Network: Redundant network interfaces and proper network infrastructure to support communication between cluster nodes and client systems.

2. Software Requirements

  • Operating System: Ensure a supported operating system is used (here is Windows Server).
  • Cluster Software: Depending on the OS:
    • Windows: Windows Failover Clustering.
  • Database: Supported database that can be clustered (e.g.,  Oracle, SQL Server).
  • SAP Software: Ensure you have the correct SAP applications that support HA.

3. Network Configuration

  • Configure a dedicated network for cluster communication (heartbeat).
  • Set up additional network interfaces for public/client communication.
  • Use static IP addresses for reliability.

4. Shared Storage Configuration

  • Set up and configure shared storage with LUNs (Logical Unit Numbers) as required by SAP and the database.
  • Ensure proper LUN masking/zoning is configured so that each server has access to the shared storage.

5. Cluster Software Installation and Configuration

  • Install the cluster software on each node.
  • Configure cluster resources such as virtual IP addresses, cluster services, and any application-specific agents (e.g., SAP-specific cluster agents).

6. SAP Installation and Configuration

  • Install SAP in a way that supports HA. This typically involves installing central services in cluster mode.
  • Configure SAP to use the shared storage for central services and the database.

7. Database Setup

  • Install and configure the database in HA mode, ensuring it uses shared storage for database files.
  • Set up database replication or log shipping as required by the specific HA setup.

8. Testing and Validation

  • Perform failover tests to ensure that the SAP system can switch over to a secondary node without significant downtime.
  • Validate that data integrity is maintained during failover.

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: MB52
entry.content: All the materials are S priced still anount is not getting posted

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud Network Security: Best Practices for IP Allowlisting and Access Control
entry.content:
When managing SAP HANA Cloud, network security is a critical responsibility for administrators. To keep your data and applications secure, SAP HANA Cloud offers flexible IP allowlisting options that let you control which IP addresses can access your instance. Currently, HANA Cloud supports three configurations for network access:

  1. Allow access only from Business Technology Platform (BTP) — the default setting.
  2. Allow access from all IP addresses — open access.
  3. Allow access only from specific, trusted IP addresses — customized access.

 

Why IP Allowlisting Matters

In a secure HANA Cloud environment, controlling who can connect to your instance is essential. Allowlisting IP addresses adds a layer of protection by limiting network access to only those addresses you deem trusted. This is especially important in production environments, where even minor security oversights can result in unintended exposure or unauthorized access.

For HANA Cloud admins, it’s recommended to use the most restrictive allowlisting option possible for their needs. Let’s explore the IP allowlisting options available and when to use each one.

1. Allow Only BTP IP Addresses

This option restricts access to IP addresses associated with Cloud Foundry within the same BTP region. When selected, only applications and tools within the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Cloud Foundry environment in the same region as your HANA Cloud instance can connect.

  • Use Case: This option is ideal for instances that exclusively interact with BTP applications or tools. For example, a HANA instance in the EU10 region will only accept connections from Cloud Foundry IPs in EU10.

  • Limitation: Tools or applications located in different regions (e.g., a tool in US10 trying to connect to EU10) will not be able to access the instance. This restriction ensures a high degree of security by preventing external access from other BTP regions.

  • Recommendation: This is the default setting and is suited for applications tightly bound to a specific regional environment within BTP.

2. Allow All IP Addresses

This option opens access to the HANA Cloud instance from any IP address, effectively allowing worldwide connectivity.

  • Use Case: Use this setting only for instances requiring global access, such as development or testing environments where multiple teams across regions may need connectivity.

  • Limitation: This is the least secure option, as it does not restrict access based on IP addresses, which can expose your instance to unwanted traffic or attacks.

  • Recommendation: It’s generally recommended to avoid this option in production environments due to the high security risk. Only use it if absolutely necessary for unrestricted access, such as in short-term or non-critical environments.

3. Allow Specific IP Addresses and IP Ranges

This option allows you to specify trusted IP addresses or IP ranges (using CIDR notation) that can access your HANA Cloud instance. With this setup, only applications or users from these allowed addresses, as well as the default Cloud Foundry IPs in your region, can connect.

  • Use Case: This option is highly recommended especially for production environments where you want to tightly control access, ensuring only known IPs can connect. For example, you might specify the IP range 192.168.1.0/24 to permit only addresses within this private range.

  • Flexibility: You can define multiple IP ranges to accommodate dynamic or changing IPs within a subnet. If Jenkins nodes or other services have dynamic IPs, consider using ranges like 172.17.0.0/24 to cover IPs that might vary across this subnet.

IP ranges are defined by CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) notation, which uses a suffix (like /24, /32, etc.) to determine how many IP addresses are included in a range. Here’s a brief overview of some common options and where you can learn more.

Common CIDR Notations and Their Ranges

  • /32: Single IP address (e.g., 192.168.1.1/32 includes only 192.168.1.1).
  • /31: Two IP addresses (typically used for point-to-point links).
  • /30: Four IP addresses (often used for very small networks).
  • /29: Eight IP addresses, e.g., 192.168.1.0 to 192.168.1.7.
  • /28: Sixteen IP addresses, e.g., 192.168.1.0 to 192.168.1.15.
  • /27: Thirty-two IP addresses, e.g., 192.168.1.0 to 192.168.1.31.
  • /26: Sixty-four IP addresses, e.g., 192.168.1.0 to 192.168.1.63.
  • /25: 128 IP addresses, e.g., 192.168.1.0 to 192.168.1.127.
  • /24: 256 IP addresses, covering a whole "C class" subnet, e.g., 192.168.1.0 to 192.168.1.255.
  • /16: 65,536 IP addresses (a full "B class" network), e.g., 192.168.0.0 to 192.168.255.255.

Where to Learn More

  1. Online CIDR Calculators:

    • Websites like CIDR.xyz or IP Calculator let you input any CIDR notation and show the full range of IPs it includes.

  2. Documentation and Tutorials:

    • Cisco's Guide to Subnetting provides thorough explanations of CIDR and subnetting: Cisco Subnetting Guide.
    • AWS Documentation on CIDR is also a great source, especially if you’re working with cloud environments: Amazon VPC Documentation on CIDR.

  • Additional Configuration for Multi-Region Access: If you need to connect from different BTP regions, be sure to add the necessary NAT IP addresses for those regions to your allowlist.

  • Recommendation: This is the best practice for production environments, as it provides the highest level of control over network access.

Summary of Recommendations for HANA Cloud Admins

SAP HANA Cloud provides flexible and secure network options for controlling instance access:

  1. Use "Allow only BTP IP addresses" for single-region setups where only Cloud Foundry applications need access.
  2. Avoid "Allow all IP addresses" in production due to its open access policy, which can expose your instance to risk.
  3. "Allow specific IP addresses and IP ranges" is recommended for production environments, where security is essential. Use CIDR notation (like 192.168.1.0/24) to define trusted IP ranges efficiently.

By implementing strict IP allowlisting, HANA Cloud admins can secure their environments while maintaining flexibility in development, testing, and production scenarios. In any setup, remember that IP allowlisting alone is one part of your security toolkit—continue to monitor and manage access as part of a comprehensive security strategy.

 

Additional Reference : 

sap-hana-cloud-network-and-security 

hana-cloud-domains-and-ip-ranges 

 

Thank you for reading this blog post!  If you have found this blog post beneficial in your learning journey, please consider giving it a ‘‘Like’ ,‘Share’ it with your friends and followers, and ‘Follow’ me for more content like this. Your support means a lot to me!
If you encounter any problems, have feedback, or need help, feel free to leave a comment or contact the SAP Community for assistance.
https://www.linkedin.com/in/showkath/

Is that possible to do? Is there any reference for this requirement?

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: Re: 4 Steps to getting SAP SuccessFactors Best Practices Content in your Customer Implementations
entry.content: Why it is mentioned "We recommend that this switch is unchecked once your implementation activities are over. Another recommendation is not to activate this switch in the Production environment." Could you please provide more details.

entry.title: SAP ERROR MESSAGE M3332
entry.content:
Hi, I can't create a material because of this message. How can i solve it? My alternative UoM is not the same as base UoM.

Diagnosis

You have entered an alternative unit of measure that is the same as the base unit of measure. These units must be different.

Procedure

Only enter an alternative unit of measure if it differs from the base unit of measure. Otherwise, leave this field blank.

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch Luzern

entry.title: Re: On what makes a great frontend framework and why UI5 is one
entry.content: Hi @frankmeertens, thanks for the feedback. And I agree!

entry.title: Re: 4.3 Web Intelligence Data Lineage Challenge
entry.content:
Hi there,

I was curious so i reproduced it and yes you are right - but for me it makes totally sense:

If you take the "Quarter" from q1, and compare it to the Quarter from q2, it is essentially the same dimension object, isn't it? If the Quarter would be a totally different dimension object, the rename would not rename both as they are different objects.

Changing the name in both cubes ensures consistency on dimension objects among the report - otherwise one can get easily lost and especially other users checking the report design would have NO idea that "q1.Quarter" could be the same Quarter Dimension object in the universe as lets say "q2.IAmNotAQuarter". So yes this is a good design decision.
In the end you have the query-Qualifier and in cells/formulars etc you have to preceed the Quarter with the query qualifier q1 or q2 in that example to be precise. If you merge that dimensions later it becomes a whole different story (but when using Joins that would not make a lot sense anyway) and then you have a common name.

And when you rename the Dimension in the join result cube "q1+q2", it has not an effect on the source dimension names in q1/q2, as there both Quarters exist with different naming, which here becomes necessary to be able to distinct them. That also makes sense.

HTH

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP , FREE_SELECTION_DAILOG , find option is needed .
entry.content: Check the parameter PFKEY of function module FREE_SELECTIONS_DIALOG. Search in the Web.

entry.title: Re: ANDROID BUILD - Build Log Error Message: Unknown error, contact support for more details.
entry.content:
Hi SavYar,

Exactly the same error and question I had, a week ago: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-discussions/android-build-error-without-log/td-p/13919329 No reaction, nor support so far.

Hope we are more lucky with your question!

Peter

entry.title: Re: Introducing the SAP Cloud SDK for AI (JavaScript/TypeScript) 🎉
entry.content:
Hey @abhishekchauhan,

there are multiple ways to integrate a hana with our clients. For example, one of our users used our langchain client together with the hana langchain client, where it is used as a vector store for embeddings: https://js.langchain.com/docs/integrations/vectorstores/hanavector/

entry.title: Re: Export Configuration CBC
entry.content:
Hi Annika,

There is no transport path or project synchronization options between the two CBC projects/Clients. The configure setup of all CBC projects/Clients are independent. This is expected standard system behavior. 

All the configure activity steps executed in the one project must be repeated for new project as well including the country, scope and organizational structure creations. Refer the relevant documentation for the 3-System Landscape and Transport Management.

Instead, the alternative is to download a zip file containing a list of configuration adaptations that have been maintained and a list of selected scopes in the current project using the Download My Configuration option. These activities can then be reused for a new project. See more Working with Projects.

 

Best Regards,

Guru Yog

entry.title: Re: XSLT Transformation and standard text for ROUTING_CREATE
entry.content: I don't understand how your XSLT converts into IDOC format. Maybe your question is more about ABAP. To convert a text into ITF format (TDFORMAT + TDLINE), use CONVERT_STREAM_TO_ITF_TEXT (many questions and answers in the Web).

entry.title: Introducing the new "Test Plan Status" filter in Test Executions app in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Running a Focused and Targeted Testing Process

In the world of software development, ensuring that your testing process remains focused and targeted is essential. Unnecessary information like test cases, which are not meant to be tested, shown to the tester can irritate and probably delay the execution. This blog post discusses the new functionality to reduce the test case list in the Test Execution app to only show test cases, which are related to an active test plan.

With this, we build on the already available functionality of using test plans for an advanced test orchestration, which is perfectly described in the related blog post Advanced Test Orchestration and Tester assignment using Test Plans in SAP Cloud ALM. Don’t miss reading it beforehand.

 

Introducing the "Test Plan Status" Filter

To help testers focus on test cases linked to an active test plan, a new filter, “Test Plan Status,” has been added to the Test Execution app. By default, this filter is set to “In Testing,” which does exclude all test cases from the execution list, which are not assigned to a test plan, or which are assigned to a finished test plan.

Along with the new filter, we have moved the “(None)” option in the Test Plan filter to the last position to ensure that customers who exclusively work with Test Plans are not inconvenienced by this entry.

 

How to Use the "Test Plan Status" Filter in Cloud ALM

For those utilizing Cloud ALM for test management, especially if you’re managing test cases only with structured test plans, this feature can be particularly useful. Here’s how you can make the most of it:

  1. Default Filter Setting: By default, the “Test Plan Status” filter is set to “In Testing.” This configuration ensures that testers focus on the cases that require attention without getting sidetracked by those not in progress or which are not associated to a test plan.

  2. Customizing the Filter: If your workflow doesn’t include a formal test plan or if you want a view of all test cases, you can modify the filter. Simply remove the default filter or set it additionally to “(None)” to include all test cases, even those outside the designated test plans.

  3. Saving Your Preferences: Once you adjust the filter settings to your needs, you can save these as a personal view. This setup allows for a customized testing view that aligns with your specific testing requirements and preferences. You can set them also as Default view, which allows a direct filtering for your preferences whenever entering the Test Execution app.

 

Conclusion

A focused testing process can make all the difference in delivering reliable, high-quality software. By leveraging the possibilities of the new “Test Plan Status” filter in Cloud ALM, testers can streamline their efforts and maintain a targeted approach.

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite -> not able to see Design -> Integr...
entry.content:
Hi Johannes,

I tried to enable this yesterday on a trial subaccount and I can see Integrations and APIs in the menu

I added these capabilities: Build Integration Scenarios and Manage API:s

.. and these role collections on my user setup for the integration suite subaccount:

1.   Integration_Provisioner
2.   PI_Administrator
3.   PI_Business_Expert
4.   PI_Integration_Developer
5.   APIManagement.Selfservice.Administrator
6.   AuthGroup.SelfService.Admin
7.   AuthGroup.API.ApplicationDeveloper
8.   APIPortal.Administrator
9.   APIPortal.Service.CatalogIntegration
10. Graph.KeyUser (if enable Graph)
11. GraphNavigator.Viewer (if enable Graph)
12. Subaccount Viewer

entry.title: SAP Community Question: In your opinion, what's the biggest use-case for Blockchain at SAP Customers
entry.content:
Good Morning SAP Community,

in your opinion what is _the_ biggest use case for Enterprise Blockchain / Distributed Ledger Technology at SAP Customers ?

Anybody following my blogs will know I like Enterprise Blockchain, and am interested in positioning Enterprise Blockchain as a Technology Standard for use-cases and business demands at SAP Customers enabling them to run Enterprise Blockchain.

I talk to a lot of people about this and regularly get asked the same question, what is _the_ BIG use case for Blockchain in the Enterprise and at SAP Customers ?

So, the floor is open, the comments are open, there is no wrong answer, and everybody is invited to give their opinion on,

for SAP Customers, what is the biggest use case for Blockchain ?

Over to you, feel welcome to put your thoughts in the comments and let's see what we discover, and, there is no wrong answer 🙂

Andy Silvey.

entry.title: Re: Remove spaces in header in IFLOW
entry.content:
Sure you can use a groovy script for do anything, csv too.

replaceAll(' ','')  remove space, you can put _  for space if you want just do replaceAll(' ','_')

def body = message.getBody(String);

body = body.replaceAll(' ','_');

message.setBody(body)

entry.title: Re: Remove spaces in header in IFLOW
entry.content: you can use chatGpt for these questions 🙂

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori - hide URL field on Zebra Scanner
entry.content:
In the config.xml, you could use the FullScreen property and set it to 1.

<Screen>
	<FullScreen value="1"/>
</Screen>

entry.title: Inquiring about maximum number of custom fields
entry.content:
What is the maximum number of custom text fields


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Inquiring about maximum number of custom fields
entry.content:
Using PDI or KUT in any client, if you create more than or equal to 744 extension fields in a standard BO node, the system displays an error message

Please find the details about Extension Field Limit in the below link.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CLOUD_APPLICATIONS_STUDIO/cbcebe3cfb1647a8b0322c18dbb0b481/75fe9c36cdf14bf09816da3acf7d94f2.html

entry.title: Re: VF31: how to excluce cancelled billing documents
entry.content: Didn't you find this answer during your search, or the solution doesn't satisfy you?

entry.title: Re: Error message when I use in enable now the function “...
entry.content:
This sounds identical to a problem users frequently face when using the SAP Cloud ALM APIs for the first time. The root cause is usually related to the SAP Cloud ALM API Service Instance not being assigned the necessary authorization scopes. It happens so frequently, we have a KBA on that topic: 3425859 - '403 Forbidden' when testing SAP Cloud ALM outbound APIs

Have you verified that the SAP Cloud ALM API Service Key you are using has the correct scopes in its Service Instance? The KBA above points to another document with instructions on how to find out what scopes are currently assigned to a Service Instance.

I checked the SAP EnableNow documentation and it mentions that at least the following scopes should be assigned to the Service Instance: 

  • calm-api.documents.read
  • calm-api.documents.write
  • calm-api.processauthoring.delete
  • calm-api.processauthoring.read
  • calm-api.processauthoring.write
  • calm-api.processmanagement.delete
  • calm-api.processmanagement.read
  • calm-api.processmanagement.write
  • calm-api.projects.read
  • calm-api.projects.write

entry.title: Re: Advanced Test Orchestration and Tester assignment using Test Plans in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
New Test Plan Status filter in Test Execution to simplify access to relevant test cases:

Introducing the new "Test Plan Status" filter in Test Executions app in SAP Cloud ALM

entry.title: Mr11 GR/IR
entry.content:
Hello, hopefully a solution can be found. 
Im having an issue during posting by using mr11. 
im trying to balance an old purchase order while  the gr document is archived. The system displays an error message “internal error in program BUILD_UP_POSTINGS_VALUES on position 5.”
Message No. CKMLGRIR00. 

Can someone assist?

Thank you.

entry.content:

Here i also included text field in custom cds view for that search help field , but still description field is not displaying in custom analytical queries.

entry.title: Re: Mr11 GR/IR
entry.content:
Hello @ben1350 

Consider the note 2655382 - MR11: Error in BUILD_UP_POSTING_VALUES If MAA and ML are active

If that doesn't resolve the problem, report it to SAP support.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP , FREE_SELECTION_DAILOG , find option is nee...
entry.content: I did try PFKEY  parameter . it is not helping in find option within my tree.

entry.title: SOLMAN TR MANAGEMENT
entry.content:
Key points:

  • TR creation
  • TR management SE01, SE09, Transport copies and logs
  • TR movement - STMS
  • TR reimport - basis
  • Recapture objects in another TR - unlock objects locked in other TR using SE03 then activate objects to capture in new T. It will ask for TR to capture the objects again.
  • TR decoupling 
  • TR failed to move with error "error occurred during post handling RS_AFTER_IMPORT" FOR *L - IT IS BECAUSE QUALITY HAS DATA LOADED IN TARGETS AS WE HAVE MADE CHANGES OR ADDED OR REMOVE FIELDS IN TRANSFORMATION THE DATA TARGETS IN QUALITY SHOULD BE CLEANED.

entry.title: Posting to two different GL accounts and also to S-Partner during Cross company bookings
entry.content:
We have a requirement where we want to post different types of cross company bookings to different GL Accounts and sometimes even directly to the S-Partner.

We have setup OBYA where we have a clearing account 123

Now, we would like to have different cases between the same combination of company codes.

Example

For cross company automatic bookings between US01 and US04

Type A automatic bookings have to be posted to account 123

For Type B to account 224

Type C directly on the S-Partner.

Is this possible

If the problem persists, please contact the system administrator(FWB 00027) 35101 The root server reported an error Initialization Failure. (Reason: CMS system database is overloaded, please try again. If the problem persists, please contact the system administrator(FWB 00027)CMS system database is overloaded, please try again. If the problem persists, please contact the system administrator(FWB 00027) CacheObjectIDs ).

entry.title: Activating the feature ID FTGL_500717 for new Resource-Related Intercompany Billing
entry.content:

1. Does the activation of the new solution will be automatically activated on Q1 2025?
2. What will happen if we will not proceed on activating the new solution? Does the old solution can still be use?
3. Is it required to activate the new solution?

entry.title: Facing error when creating MTO Production Order
entry.content:
Dear SAP Communitiy members,

I'm testing the process of MTO in our partner system, but we are facing error when checking the Material in the production order.

 



This is the error message that I'm facing now.

When I jump to configuration side, 

Availability key both 1 and 2 is exists in our system.
Can you give any advice why I'm facing this error when creating production order?

(I'm testing in the partner environment, so Availability key configuration side is provided by SAP)

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Hello @RajKishoreUppala welcome to SAP Community. As you are tuning in from the US, I would like to point to the already existing Location Group in Americas. These groups are designed to connect local people. There are also physical events from time to time for knowledge sharing and networking.

Furthermore, these video series might help you to understand the SAP Community better: Discover the SAP Community - How-To Video Series

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content:
Glad to have you as such an experienced person on board @RajKishoreUppala 

I also mentioned some tipps & tricks in my comment to your other reply. See: https://community.sap.com/t5/welcome-corner-discussions/sap-community-where-are-you-from/m-p/13936708/highlight/true#M9670 😊

entry.title: Control Restart Condition of Flexible workflow for Purchase Contract in S/4HANA (Public cloud)
entry.content:
flexible workflow for Purchase Contract in SAP S/4HANA Public cloud Edition.

Workflow setting in "Manage Workflow for Purchase Contract" App is configured for Document type WK Value Contract. Approver determination is provided as specific set of users.

I have tested below situations where WF is triggered/Restarted even for Approved Contracts.

Business requirement is to ONLY start the workflow in case of Contract target value is INCREASED. Rest of all Standard conditions which SAP is considering needs to be ignored for Workflow Retrigger/Restart.

 

Contract value increased

WF triggered

Contract value decreased

WF triggered

Contract item added

WF triggered

Contract item deleted

WF triggered

Contract item Qty changed

WF not triggered

Contract header Incoterm changed

WF triggered

Supplier Material number changed

WF triggered

Contract description changed

WF triggered

Purchasing group changed

WF triggered

Payment term changed

WF triggered

Validity end date changed

WF triggered

Contract item description changed

WF triggered

Attachment added

WF triggered

Enabled Edit mode --> click Save

WF triggered

I have already checked Purchase Contract workflow restart conditions configuration where I dont see other conditions to control (As these are the only SAP Std delivered Conditions)

 

Do I need to achieve this Via BAdI - MM_PUR_S4_CTR_WFL_RESTART (Definition of Conditions to Restart Workflow in Purchase Contracts).

Is there any Standard WF Setting I need to maintain to achieve this requirement ?

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP , FREE_SELECTION_DAILOG , find option is needed .
entry.content: You mean FREE_SELECTIONS_DIALOG I suppose

entry.title: Re: EWM x DM integration - PSA stock not read
entry.content:
Hi,
Actually i don't get any message in SLG1 or STR_UTIL (transcation to check the web service message output).
I've done both the web service configuration and the customizing as the hel giude suggest: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/9020575faae645b8ae3679f93bd2e075.html?locale=en-US

I've also tried to put breakpoints through out the Badi, but it seems not to be called form the EWM-DM interface (i'm talking about this Badi /SCWM/EX_EWM_HU_CHG and this two enhanchement spot /SCWM/ESI_EWM_HU_CHG and /SCWM/ESI_STOCK_MOVEMENT_NOTIF).

As you can see my stock is set as Syncronized:

And my storage type are set as K.

If you have any suggestion, it would be great!

SS

entry.title: message no M7042. Batch does not exist?
entry.content:

I have an issue. I would like to close old inspection lots for incoming goods, but if I use transaction QA11 then message no. 7042 comes, that the batch does not exist ?
How can I close it? In the transaction MSC1N I cannot define batch too.
We are speaking about very old inspection lots.

Thanks a lot for your support.

Br:

david

BCALV_TEST_GRID

Expand and collapse total lines in ABAP ALV grid d... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP , FREE_SELECTION_DAILOG , find option is nee...
entry.content: Do not use Answer to respond to a comment or answer. Answer is intended to be used to propose a solution to the initial question. Use Comment on the initial question or on any answer instead.

entry.title: Re: How to add Subtotal button to ALV IDA toolbar report?
entry.content: Hello did anyone find how to handle that collapse/expand button or subtotal?

entry.title: Re: SAP Service and Asset Manager Licensing: What You Need to Know about Mobile Services Entitlement
entry.content:
Dear Alba,

Thanks for this valuable summary.

In the solution diagram, Dynamic Forms are shown in a subaccount separate from the subaccount for Mobile Services. Why is that? Is it not possible to deploy Dynamic Forms in the same subaccount as the Mobile Services? Or is it that you want to highlight that these could be different? One of the installation prerequisites listed in the documentation is "To enable end users to work with forms created by SAP Dynamic Forms, the SAP Dynamic Forms application must be installed in the same SAP BTP Subaccount as the SAP Service and Asset Manager application, used by the end user. is installed on for use by the end user.".  The picture here appears to be contradicting the documentation.  

The use of the term "internal SAP Applications" for referring to "SAP Service & Asset manager" is a little confusing. These are SAP applications to that are generally available for customers. So why qualify these as internal? In the Mobile Services these are called SAP Applications.  

Regards,

Dinu

entry.title: Re: Proposal value field on compensation sheet
entry.content: Xavier! Thank you very much for the information! It is very useful to me!

entry.title: Re: Mr11 GR/IR
entry.content: Thank you. This note refers to “position 2” and not “position 5”. Can you refer to note that relates to my issue? Thank you.

entry.title: Re: F-32 clear customer: Error- Enter the Item number
entry.content:
Hello,

No problem, thank you anyway for your effort. One of SD colleague found out that both down payment items were canceled in the customer order. This means, the order is no longer relevant for down payments in the system and the header cannot be used for reconciliation. 

Best Regards,

Raman

entry.title: Commerce PO in Ariba Invoice Management Solution from Network
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Can someone confirm if you have worked on a scenario where a PO originated from non-sap system is sent to Network via Commerce Automation and if we have subscription for Ariba Invoice Management solution instead of Full suite of B&I, is it possible to get the PO copy from Network to Ariba Invoice Management solution? This is to check if we could perform the 2-way or 3-way match in Ariba Invoice Management solution. 

If this is possible, can you please confirm where do we need to look for such kind of configuration? Is it in Ariba Network, Document Routing configuration?

Any leads on this would be much appreciated!

Thanks,
Velu

entry.title: Re: Upload Plan data using a ‘Starter Button’ in SAC Story from a File (Excel/CSV)
entry.content:
HI Vereendra, 

Thanks for the blog. 

One issue we are finding with the new Upload Starter is that it does not list the error records, which is problematic because end users have no way to understand what's going on. 

In your blog you mention "If there are rejection of records, users can download and correct them", but we don't see this on the data management log:

Is this expected or maybe a limitation? The reason why it fails is because we are uploading a file with new member on a private dimension, so we would expect the system to list the rejected record. 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Configuring reciever port for outbound IDOC
entry.content:
Did you read (and apply) SAP suggestion from online documentation

  • Look for 'This wiki provides details on keeping ALE settings, for example the Distribution mode (BD64), ports in Idoc processing (WE21), and Partner Profiles WE20.'

entry.title: Re: XSLT Transformation and standard text for ROUTING_CREATE
entry.content: Do you want to generate (by transformation) another XML file ready to use with IDOC_XML_FROM_FILE or similar tool?

entry.title: SAP Enable Now und IAS
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have configured IAS as a proxy and am provisioning users from Azure to IAS and then to SAP Enable Now Azure->IAS->. How can roles be automatically assigned to provisioned users in Enable Now ?

Is there something similar to groups/teams in SAP Enable Now, like in SAC, that can be granted access via a role?

Unfortunately, I can't find any information on this.

Many Thanks for any help!
